mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 240 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in co-expansion periods show Rank IC < 0.03, or individual series show no correlation with SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening indicated by declining unemployment (UNRATE) below 4% reduces wage pressures and stabilizes corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances with UNRATE < 4%, or Rank IC < 0.02 in tight labor regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals expanding employment, increasing consumer spending, and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 50%+ of PAYEMS > 150k instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 in expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1100 signals weak demand for construction, reducing sectoral growth and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances with HOUST < 1100, or Rank IC > 0.05 in contractionary demand periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and RSAFS indicate monetary tightening coupled with strong consumer demand, creating mixed signals that reduce SPX forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show Rank IC > 0.03 in co-occurrence of FEDFUNDS and RSAFS increases, or individual series show no directional correlation.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) above 450k billion signals sustained demand, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 50%+ of RSAFS > 450k instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 in high-demand periods.
